method_atom: A structural correspondence between two multi-component systems established by exhibiting a single polynomial invariant of a shared symmetry group — a hyperdeterminant or the invariant of an exceptional group acting on a vector of charges/amplitudes — that simultaneously quantifies a key scalar property of each system, so their classifications map onto one another exactly. The components are indexed and their combination rules fixed by the multiplication table of a division algebra encoded in a finite incidence geometry (a projective plane of imaginary units), which decomposes the invariant and the component set into algebraically distinguished sub-blocks.
object_atom: On one side, the entropy of extremal black holes (and black strings) in string theory and supergravity — the 8-charge STU black hole of N=2 and the 56-charge N=8 black hole, realized microscopically as intersecting D3-branes wrapping the six compact dimensions T^6. On the other, the multipartite quantum entanglement of three and seven qubits and of three qutrits in quantum information theory.
brief_reason: What carries across is the invariant-theoretic machinery — a shared group invariant measuring a property of a multi-component system, organized by division-algebra/finite-geometry combinatorics — while the domain-specific content is the two concrete systems being linked (string-theory black holes vs. quantum-information entanglement), which the paper unavoidably fuses into a single correspondence.
